MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-11-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF DEPARTMENT OF FAIR EMPLOYMENT AND HOUSING's MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint.

DFEH's motion for leave to file the first amended complaint is GRANTED. As the court previously ruled, the DFEH can seek recovery of punitive damages in the employment context and Forever 21 overstates the holding of Dyna-Med, Inc. v. Fair Emp. & Housing Comm. (1987) 43 Cal.3d 1379. In that case, the Supreme Court limited its review specifically to the issue of what types of damages the Fair Employment Housing Commission (i.e., the agency) could impose. The Legislature amended FEHA and disbanded the Commission. Since the court-not an agency-is adjudicating the punitives issue, the DFEH can seek recovery of that remedy. Gov't Code 12965(a) allows the DFEH to bring a claim on behalf of real parties. Gov't Code 12965(c) states that "[a] court may grant as relief in any action filed pursuant to subdivision (a) any relief a court is empowered to grant in a civil action brought pursuant to subdivision (b), in addition to any other relief that, in the judgment of the court, will effectuate the purpose of this part." (See also Commodore Home Systems v. Superior Court (1982) 32 Cal.3d 211, 215 (all forms of relief granted to civil litigants generally (including punitive damages where appropriate) may be awarded by court in civil action).) Allowing the amendment does not prejudice Forever 21 as it already brought a motion for summary adjudication that attacked the substantive merits of the punitive damages claim and the trial date is unchanged.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, torreanom@aol.com, Reported. (D302/RU)
